note: digital-me-os metric-truth sweep (motus-data-sweep). Surfaces = the LIVE OA dashboard metrics API (port 3458; override via DATA_SWEEP_DASHBOARD_URL for a locally-served fixed build) + the daily digest's staging step (regenerated at capture time by the product's own --stage entrypoint, no LLM). Truths = the flat ~/digital-me/{wiki,tastes} trees (frontmatter `created` chronology via health-sweep/bin/count-fm-created.py) — the trees ARE the primary source post-§A unification. wiki-total carries pct tolerance 5: intake counts only yaml-parseable entries and old distribution rows for retired domains are never scrubbed — a known, named drift class (advisory), not silent fudge. Born from the 2026-07-03 incident pair: dashboard taste flow read 0 while leaves landed on disk; digest said Wiki 0 on a 29-entry day.

metrics.note: distribution snapshot: Σ tastes totals must equal the taste leaves on disk

distribution snapshot: Σ wiki totals vs wiki entries on disk. pct 5 names the accepted drift: yaml-unparseable entries are invisible to intake, and distribution rows for retired domains linger with old as_of.
flow, THE 2026-07-03 incident surface: taste creations shown for the last 2 days (UTC window matches sqlite date('now','-2 days')) vs leaves actually created on disk in the same window
flow, week window: taste creations shown vs leaves created on disk
THE 2026-07-03 digest incident surface: staged tldr.wiki_new for yesterday vs wiki entries with frontmatter created == yesterday (PT — both sides use date -v-1d)

digest taste lane sanity: staged tldr.taste_total is log-derived (dream-cycle materialization), so no exact tree pairing exists — this metric only refuses the zero-lie: taste_total 0 while leaves were created yesterday is a D1. required:false — the lane depends on the nightly log's presence.
